key_insights.insight: Society overvalues the left brain's logical, analytical functions, creating imbalance and contributing to societal problems.
key_insights.quote: We are skewed as a society to the two parts of the left brain, which focuses on me, the individual... We get in trouble when this is the only portion of our brain that we value, because look at the world we currently live in.

key_insights.insight: We have four distinct, anatomically structured parts of our brain, each with different skill sets, that manifest as different personalities.
key_insights.quote: We have these four different anatomically, neuroanatomically structured parts of our brain, and we can pick and choose who and how we want to be in any moment when we know what our choices are.

key_insights.insight: Trauma's purpose is to inform you and protect you in the present moment, not to become a lifestyle.
key_insights.quote: The purpose of trauma is to say to you, you're a biological creature. You're in the present moment. You're a real human being. You have a life. My life, part of my life is my trauma...The trauma is supposed to be information. We get in trouble when we turn it into a lifestyle.

key_insights.insight: Emotions are designed to last only 90 seconds; prolonged emotional states are maintained by re-triggering the initial thought.
key_insights.quote: From the moment you think of thought, we're only doing three things inside of our brain at any moment in time. We think thoughts, we feel emotions, and we run physiological loops to what we're thinking and feeling... if you simply observe it, it will loop right through just like a muscle reflex. It's an emotional reflex, less than 90 seconds.

key_insights.insight: The right hemisphere focuses on the present moment and connection to the whole, promoting peace and hope.
key_insights.quote: The right brain is right here right now...That's the beauty of the right hemisphere. It is hope. It's possibility.

key_insights.insight: Understanding and nurturing our brain cells is key to manifesting mental health.
key_insights.quote: When we understand the brain cells and what they do and how to work with them and how to keep them well, then we can manifest our own mental health.

key_insights.insight: The odds of your existence are astronomical and are in themselves something to marvel at.
key_insights.quote: Think about this… the little egg cell that would evolve into you eventually, it took form… And how can that not be something that we celebrate the wonder of the odds you had to beat just to be here?

actionable_advice.advice: Practice self-awareness to identify which of your 'four characters' is dominant in different situations.
actionable_advice.how_to: Observe yourself in different situations. When are you logical and organized (Character One)? When are you reacting to past pain (Character Two)? When are you playful and in the present moment (Character Three)? When are you accessing your inner wisdom and peace (Character Four)?

actionable_advice.advice: Prioritize sleep to allow brain cells to cleanse waste and repair.
actionable_advice.how_to: Make sleep a non-negotiable priority. Aim for consistent sleep schedule

actionable_advice.advice: Pay attention to your diet, prioritizing fresh fruits, vegetables, and limiting preservatives and sugar.
actionable_advice.how_to: Be mindful of food labels and choose whole, unprocessed foods as often as possible.

actionable_advice.advice: Engage in movement and activities that bring you joy and connect you with your body.
actionable_advice.how_to: Identify activities that you genuinely enjoy and make time for them regularly.  Don't let the "Helen" (logical brain) talk you out of it.

actionable_advice.advice: When experiencing trauma, acknowledge it and pull energy into other parts of your brain for support, acknowledging trauma should be seen as information and not as a lifestyle.
actionable_advice.how_to: Acknowledge the trauma but engage in other areas of the brain as well - wisdom to soothe yourself and joy to create a positive reinforcement.
Take a 30 second pause. Don't try to squeeze yourself into a box.

memorable_stories: Dr. Taylor's stroke experience and subsequent eight-year recovery, including regaining her ability to understand numbers.
Dr. Taylor's early experiences dissecting roadkill with her aunt.
The story of the physicians drawing hopscotch outside the ER to bring back a moment of joy.
Jill's detailed story of the origins of your DNA, and how you began as a tiny cell and how the cells in your body multiplied over time.
contrarian_claims: It is important to be at one with and embrace pain and sadness, that we are 'wired' for and should celebrate, and not avoid.
Wanting more can create negative energy, we must first care about connection.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - No.1 Brain Scientist: Your Brain Is Lying To You! Here's How I Discovered The Truth!
